title: daviesia decurrens subsp. decurrens
intro: Daviesia decurrens subsp. decurrens, also known as the hairy daviesia or hairy daviesia bush, is a plant species native to Australia. It is a flowering shrub in the Fabaceae family, valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its use in traditional Aboriginal medicine. The plant is known to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate various health issues, including fever, coughs, and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Daviesia decurrens subsp. decurrens can be used as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
